Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria, and low urine specific gravity, indicating significant renal impairment. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor) and elevated urinary bilirubin further support renal dysfunction. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, excluding diabetes as a cause. Electrolyte disturbances (high chloride) are present. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.
